Infrastructure_type: poultry shelter or coop, system unspecified
Infra_water_electricity_note: ESPAC livestock modules do not expose direct infrastructure/water/electricity variables for this row; literature/system proxy attached
Infra_water_electricity_citations: Producción sostenible en avicultura - Reports 222.03 L per 1000 hens per day, equivalent to 0.222 L/hen/day; used as the preferred practical water proxy for unspecified poultry rows. (https://dokumen.pub/produccion-sostenible-en-avicultura-l-6359269.html) | FAO small-scale poultry production - Reports that a laying hen can consume up to about 0.25 L/day of water, supporting the adopted 0.222 L/hen/day proxy. (https://www.fao.org/4/y4628e/y4628e03.htm) | Iowa layer-house electricity summary - A reduced poultry-house electricity proxy was retained as a fallback where ESPAC does not distinguish the shelter or lighting setup and no stronger Ecuador, Latin American, or FAO per-bird electricity factor was identified. (https://blogs.millersville.edu/aest/2022/05/13/dr-haughery-publishes-research-findings/)
Reference_output_basis_note: Egg counts converted to kg shell eggs using 0.056699 kg/egg; live-weight-equivalent per kg shell eggs is based on total flock live weight divided by total egg output.
Diet_data_status: no diet share data in ESPAC; literature proxy attached from config
Ration_composition_note: no diet/ration fields found in avian module; literature proxy diet attached from inputs/02-05_espac_lci_coefficients.yml; main feed columns mapped from proxy diet as common compound feed, with no pasture or waste component
Diet_proxy_note: Semipasture or free-range scavenging was not quantified in the source; proxy covers balanced feed only.

Infrastructure_type: grazing goat holding with shelter and trough
Infra_water_electricity_note: ESPAC livestock modules do not expose direct infrastructure/water/electricity variables for this row; literature/system proxy attached
Infra_water_electricity_citations: FAO-linked livestock watering structures guideline - Reports ewe or doe water requirements around 9.5 L/day and milking ewe or doe requirements around 13.2 L/day; 9.5 L/head/day was adopted as a general goat live-animal proxy. (https://www.fao.org/fileadmin/user_upload/emergencies/docs/UNEP_FAO_South_Sudan_WH_Structures_guidelines.pdf)
Reference_output_basis_note: Animals_total_head multiplied by representative live weight, then converted to estimated meat output with the configured carcass-yield proxy.
Diet_data_status: no diet share data in ESPAC; literature proxy attached from config
Ration_composition_note: no diet/ration fields found in other livestock module; literature proxy diet attached from inputs/02-05_espac_lci_coefficients.yml; main feed columns mapped from proxy diet as unmatched pasture for forage plus browse, with mineral or compound feed retained separately
Diet_proxy_note: Composite proxy. Total intake is a fallback mature-doe dry matter intake example; tree-forage share is based on Ecuadorian caprine preference data and native browse literature from the Ecuadorian Sierra.
Diet_proxy_citations: Evaluación de la preferencia de consumo de leguminosas arbóreas con potencial forrajero en rumiantes menores - Reports caprine Acacia melanoxylon voluntary intake of 211.07 g/kg MS and 20.155 g/kg PV^0.75 at 23 kg live weight. (https://repositorio.uta.edu.ec/items/770d67bf-97e2-485a-8227-d941b7f4e1b2) | Cabras Lecheras Selección y Hábitos de Consumo de Plantas Nativas en la Sierra Ecuatoriana - Ecuador review identifying native browse and shrub resources as a central component of dairy goat feeding in the Sierra. (https://polodelconocimiento.com/ojs/index.php/es/article/download/7592/pdf) | Goat Nutrition - Provides a mature-doe dry matter intake example of about 1.011 kg/day (2.23 lb/day) used as fallback total intake. (https://sfyl.ifas.ufl.edu/duval/agriculture-and-agribusiness-management/livestock-and-poultry/goats/goat-nutrition/)

Infra_water_electricity_note: ESPAC livestock modules do not expose direct infrastructure/water/electricity variables for this row; literature/system proxy attached
Infra_water_electricity_citations: FAO Rural structures in the tropics - Table 10.15 reports broiler drinking-water requirements around 0.20 to 0.35 L/bird/day at 9 to 12 weeks; the midpoint 0.275 L/bird/day was adopted. (https://www.fao.org/4/s1250e/S1250E0q.htm) | Iowa layer-house electricity summary - In the absence of a stronger broiler electricity factor in ESPAC, a conservative small-poultry-house electricity proxy of 0.005 kWh/bird/day was retained. (https://blogs.millersville.edu/aest/2022/05/13/dr-haughery-publishes-research-findings/)
Reference_output_basis_note: Animals_total_head multiplied by representative live weight, then converted to estimated meat output with the configured carcass-yield proxy.

Diet_proxy_note: Broiler feed proxy based on FAO cumulative feed demand of 4 to 6 kg per bird to reach 2 kg live weight by 9 to 12 weeks, converted to an average daily intake.
Diet_proxy_citations: FAO Rural structures in the tropics - Reports that one broiler grown from day-old chick to 2 kg live weight at 9 to 12 weeks requires 4 to 6 kg feed; the midpoint was annualized to about 0.068 kg/bird/day. (https://www.fao.org/4/s1250e/S1250E0q.htm)

Infrastructure_type: grazing ovine holding with shelter and trough
Infra_water_electricity_note: ESPAC livestock modules do not expose direct infrastructure/water/electricity variables for this row; literature/system proxy attached
Infra_water_electricity_citations: FAO-linked livestock watering structures guideline - Reports feeder lamb and kid water requirements around 7.6 L/day and adult ewe or doe requirements above that level; 7.6 L/head/day was retained as a conservative live-animal proxy for mixed ovine rows. (https://www.fao.org/fileadmin/user_upload/emergencies/docs/UNEP_FAO_South_Sudan_WH_Structures_guidelines.pdf)
Reference_output_basis_note: Animals_total_head multiplied by representative live weight, then converted to estimated meat output with the configured carcass-yield proxy.
Diet_data_status: no diet share data in ESPAC; literature proxy attached from config
Ration_composition_note: no diet/ration fields found in ovine module; literature proxy diet attached from inputs/02-05_espac_lci_coefficients.yml; main feed columns mapped from proxy diet as unmatched pasture for forage plus browse, with mineral or compound feed retained separately
Diet_proxy_note: Composite Ecuador proxy. Total intake is based on an ovine feeding trial; tree-forage share comes from Acacia mearnsii voluntary intake; mineral intake comes from a Blackbelly sheep grazing study in the Ecuadorian Amazon.
Diet_proxy_citations: UTA thesis by Erika Esthefanía Lluay Guilcapi (bitstream 32507) - Reports voluntary dry matter intake of 1268.85 g/day in weaned sheep in Ecuador. (https://repositorio.uta.edu.ec/bitstream/123456789/32507/1/Tesis%20182%20Medicina%20Veterinaria%20y%20Zootecnia%20-Lluay%20Guilcapi%20Erika%20Esthefan%C3%ADa.pdf) | Evaluación de la preferencia de consumo de leguminosas arbóreas con potencial forrajero en rumiantes menores - Reports ovine Acacia mearnssi voluntary intake of 272.50 g/kg MS and 12.60 g/kg PV^0.75 at 60.44 kg live weight. (https://repositorio.uta.edu.ec/items/770d67bf-97e2-485a-8227-d941b7f4e1b2) | Effect of Mineral Supplementation on the Macromineral Concentration in Blood in Pre- and Postpartum Blackbelly Sheep - Reports 0.005 kg per animal per day mineral supplementation under free-grazing conditions in the Ecuadorian Amazon. (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7401505/)

Infrastructure_type: Ecuador smallholder pigsty or swine pen with feeder and drinker
Infra_water_electricity_note: ESPAC livestock modules do not expose direct infrastructure/water/electricity variables for this row; literature/system proxy attached
Infra_water_electricity_citations: FAO Livestock housing for pigs - Pig water consumption varies by class from about 2.5 L/day at 25 kg to 7.0 L/day at 90 to 120 kg; 7.0 L/head/day was adopted as a practical market-pig proxy for mixed ESPAC swine rows. (https://www.fao.org/3/s1250e/S1250E14.htm) | Caracterización de granjas porcinas de Banasur, Pasaje, El Oro - Provides Ecuador smallholder swine-system context and supports the use of a simple pen-based infrastructure proxy, although it does not provide a clean biological drinking-water factor. (https://polodelconocimiento.com/ojs/index.php/es/article/view/1043/html) | National Hog Farmer study summary - Finishing-barn electricity around 11.2 kWh per finished pig produced was annualized over a 180-day finishing period to derive a daily proxy. (https://www.nationalhogfarmer.com/livestock-management/study-goes-to-pig-barns-to-find-actual-energy-use)
Reference_output_basis_note: Animals_total_head multiplied by representative live weight, then converted to estimated meat output with the configured carcass-yield proxy.
